solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int maxVowels(string s, int k) {
        int maxVowels = 0, currentVowels = 0;
        unordered_set<char> vowels = {'a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u'};
        
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); ++i) {
            if (vowels.count(s[i])) currentVowels++;
            if (i >= k && vowels.count(s[i - k])) currentVowels--;
            maxVowels = max(maxVowels, currentVowels);
        }
        
        return maxVowels;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a sliding window approach to efficiently count vowels in substrings of length k. It maintains a running count of vowels in the current window and updates it as the window slides. This avoids recalculating the number of vowels from scratch for each window, resulting in linear time complexity. The space complexity is constant as only a few variables are used.
